name: @radzor/guardrails
description: Validate LLM inputs and outputs against configurable safety rules including PII detection, content filtering, prompt injection detection, and length limits. Provides a rule engine for building custom validation pipelines.

inputs.name: enableBuiltinRules
inputs.description: Whether to load built-in rules (PII, prompt injection, profanity) on initialization.

inputs.name: maxInputLength
inputs.description: Maximum allowed character length for input text.

inputs.name: maxOutputLength
inputs.description: Maximum allowed character length for output text.

outputs.name: validationResult
outputs.description: Result of validating text against the active rules.

fields.name: passed
fields.description: Whether the text passed all validation rules.

fields.name: violations
fields.type: Array<{ rule: string; severity: string; message: string; matches?: string[] }>
fields.description: List of rule violations found in the text.

events.name: onViolation
events.description: Fired each time a rule violation is detected.

actions.name: validateInput

params.name: text
params.description: The input text (user prompt) to validate.

actions.description: Validate input text against all active rules.

actions.name: validateOutput

params.name: text
params.description: The output text (LLM response) to validate.

actions.description: Validate output text against all active rules.

llm.constraints: No API keys required — all validation is local. PII detection uses regex patterns and is not 100% accurate. For production PII handling, consider combining with a dedicated PII service.
